author: Brian Leslie
message:
Remove retired non-Anthropic models; restore Legacy Bedrock Sonnets

Remove models their providers have shut down: open_ai_o1_mini (OpenAI
retired o1-mini 2025-10-27), google_gemini_3_0_pro (gemini-3-pro-preview
shut down 2026-03-09, alias redirects to 3.1), and the OpenRouter keys
open_router_gemini_2_0_flash, open_router_gemini_3_pro_preview,
open_router_grok_4, and open_router_grok_4_1_fast (gone from
OpenRouter's model list).

Update google_gemini_2_5_pro to the stable gemini-2.5-pro API name in
place of the retired preview snapshot.

Restore bedrock_claude_3_7_sonnet and bedrock_claude_3_5_sonnet, which
PR #501 removed early: AWS lists them as Legacy with EOL 2026-07-30,
and correct the CHANGELOG accordingly.

Sync the install generator's initializer template and setup.md model
lists with the registry.

author: Ben Roesch
message:
Merge pull request #501 from CultivateLabs/feature/remove-deprecated-anthropic-models

Remove retired Claude models from the LLM registry

author: Brian Leslie
message: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into feature/remove-deprecated-anthropic-models

author: Brian Leslie
message:
Remove retired Claude models from the LLM registry

Anthropic has retired six Claude models on its API (Claude 4 Opus/Sonnet, 3.7 Sonnet, 3.5 Sonnet/Haiku, 3 Opus), so requests to them now fail; their AWS Bedrock equivalents are past end-of-life on Bedrock, and OpenRouter's claude-3.7-sonnet is gone. Removes the registry keys anthropic_claude_4_opus, anthropic_claude_4_sonnet, anthropic_claude_3_7_sonnet, anthropic_claude_3_5_sonnet, anthropic_claude_3_5_haiku, anthropic_claude_3_opus, bedrock_claude_4_opus, bedrock_claude_3_7_sonnet, bedrock_claude_3_5_sonnet, bedrock_claude_3_5_haiku, bedrock_claude_3_opus, and open_router_claude_3_7_sonnet.

Bedrock sets its own retirement schedule, so bedrock_claude_4_sonnet (still available there) and bedrock_claude_4_1_opus are kept; anthropic_claude_4_1_opus is also kept (deprecated but functional until its 2026-08-05 retirement).

Migrates the adapter, batch, job, and admin specs that used a removed model as their default onto surviving models (Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.5, Bedrock/OpenRouter Sonnet 5); anthropic_spec uses Opus 4.1 so its json_response-tool fallback tests still exercise a model without native structured-output support. The anthropic/ and bedrock/ VCR cassettes are edited in place — only the request model id and token limit are swapped to match the surviving models, leaving the recorded responses unchanged.

Also updates the install-generator template, setup and evals docs, and locale model names, and adds a CHANGELOG entry linking the providers' deprecation pages.

author: Ben Roesch
message:
Merge pull request #500 from CultivateLabs/feature/model-completion-authorizer

Add Raif.config.model_completion_authorizer hook

author: Ben Roesch
message:
Propagate model_completion_authorizer vetoes through Raif::Task.run

Address Codex review feedback on PR #500: an authorizer veto raised
inside Raif::Llm#chat was swallowed by Raif::Task.run's rescue
StandardError, so callers could not distinguish an intentional
authorization denial from an ordinary model failure.

Llm#chat now tags any exception raised by the authorizer with the
Raif::Errors::ModelCompletionAuthorizationError marker module
(preserving the original class and message). Task.run marks the task
failed and then re-raises the tagged veto to its synchronous caller
instead of logging/reporting it as a model failure.

Conversation responses run in Raif::ConversationEntryJob with no host
caller to receive a raise, so a veto there continues to surface as a
failed Raif::ConversationEntry rather than propagating.

author: Ben Roesch
message:
feature/model-completion-authorizer: Add Raif.config.model_completion_authorizer hook

Optional lambda called with llm:/source: keyword args at the start of
Raif::Llm#chat, before the ModelCompletion is created or any provider API
call is made (and before the llm_api_requests_enabled guard). Raise from
it to veto the request. Lets host apps enforce per-account usage limits,
including stopping in-progress agent runs.

author: Brian Leslie
message:
Don't claim structured output support for new Bedrock Claude models

AWS model cards mark structured outputs as unsupported on the Bedrock
endpoints for Claude Sonnet 5, Opus 4.8, and Fable 5. With
supports_structured_outputs: true, the Bedrock adapter sends
output_config.text_format and skips the json_response tool fallback, so
JSON-schema calls would fail. Drop the flag until AWS lists support.

author: Brian Leslie
message:
Add latest LLM models to the registry

Register the mid-2026 model releases and their display names:

- Anthropic: Claude Sonnet 5, Opus 4.8, and Fable 5 (direct API, AWS Bedrock, and OpenRouter)
- OpenAI: GPT-5.5 (Completions + Responses) and GPT-5.5 Pro
- Google: Gemini 3.5 Flash (direct API and OpenRouter)

Pricing, context windows, and API names follow the current provider docs. Note that Fable 5 requires provider_data_share on Bedrock, which AWS has since restricted; the direct and OpenRouter entries work.

author: Ben Roesch
message:
Merge pull request #498 from CultivateLabs/bugfix/strip-null-bytes-conversation-history

Strip NUL bytes from agent conversation history before persisting

author: Ben Roesch
message:
Strip NUL bytes from agent conversation history before persisting

Tool results carry arbitrary external content (fetched pages, search
results) that can contain NUL bytes, which Postgres cannot store in a
jsonb column. A single poisoned tool result would abort the agent's
save! with PG::UntranslatableCharacter, failing the whole run.

Deep-strip NUL bytes from each conversation history entry before
persisting, protecting all agent types at the single chokepoint.

author: Brian Leslie
message:
Show each completion's requested tool calls on the admin agent page

Add a "Tool calls" column to the Model Completions table on the agent show
page, backed by a new Raif::ModelCompletion#tool_call_summary that combines
developer-managed calls (response_tool_calls) and provider-managed calls
(provider_managed_tool_calls, e.g. OpenAI/Anthropic web search). A batch
rejected over max_tool_calls_per_iteration persists no tool invocations, so
the agent page previously gave no indication of what the model requested; the
requested calls are now visible there.

author: Brian Leslie
message:
State the per-step tool-call cap in the agent prompt and corrective feedback

When max_tool_calls_per_iteration is set, NativeToolCallingAgent's default
system prompt now states the limit ("you may make up to N independent tool
calls in a single step") instead of an unbounded "several", so the model
self-limits rather than over-fanning and getting the whole batch rejected.
The over-cap corrective feedback is reworded to keep inviting batching up to
the cap and tell the model how to recover. Both read correctly at a cap of 1
(singular wording) and are unchanged when the cap is nil, so the default-prompt
spec is unaffected.
